CREATE TABLE employees (id INT, name VARCHAR, department VARCHAR, salary INT);
SELECT name, salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS salary_rank FROM employees;
CREATE TABLE departments (dept_id INT, dept_name VARCHAR);
SELECT d.dept_name, e.name, e.salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY d.dept_name ORDER BY e.salary DESC) AS salary_rank FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department = d.dept_name;
SELECT id, name, department, salary, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY salary DESC) AS overall_rank FROM employees WHERE department != 'Sales';
SELECT name, department, salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary) AS salary_rank FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees);
SELECT name, salary, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY department, salary DESC) AS ranked FROM employees WHERE salary IN (SELECT DISTINCT salary FROM employees WHERE salary > 70000);
CREATE TABLE projects (project_id INT, employee_id INT, project_name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1, 1, 'Project A'), (2, 2, 'Project B'), (3, 2, 'Project C'), (4, 3, 'Project D');
SELECT e.name, COUNT(p.project_id) AS project_count, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(p.project_id) DESC) AS project_rank FROM employees e LEFT JOIN projects p ON e.id = p.employee_id GROUP BY e.name;
